# Synthetic Campaign Workspace Brain Dump

This fixture contains synthetic, non-sensitive data only.

## Workspace purpose
Create a weekly community campaign update from public notes.

## Historical background
The workspace began as a one-off update, then expanded to event notices, volunteer reminders, sponsor acknowledgements, social captions, board summaries, and archive notes. The original operator preferred short drafts. A later operator requested longer narrative summaries. Another operator asked for bullets first and prose second. These historical preferences are all kept here even though they apply to different outputs.

## Audience notes
Residents want plain language. Volunteers want dates and assignments. Sponsors want acknowledgement. Board members want risks and metrics. New members need definitions. Long-term members dislike repeated explanations. Readers may use email, print, mobile, or social media.

## Writing rules
Use active voice. Avoid jargon. Define acronyms. Prefer short paragraphs. Include a title. Include a summary. Include details. Include context. Include a call to action. Avoid excessive calls to action. Sound friendly but formal. Sound concise but comprehensive. Never omit useful background. Do not make the update too long.

## Workflow history
The old workflow started with research, then drafting, then review. The current workflow sometimes begins with intake. Urgent announcements may skip research. Sponsor updates may need finance review. Event updates may need venue review. Volunteer updates may need coordinator review. The exact active workflow is not identified here.

## Output conventions
Past outputs were stored in `output/`, `drafts/`, `reports/`, `archive/`, and occasionally the root. Markdown is preferred, except when plain text, JSON, CSV, or presentation format is needed. Filenames have used dates, slugs, issue numbers, and labels such as `final-final`.

## Quality checklist
Check facts, dates, names, links, tone, spelling, audience fit, length, formatting, calls to action, ownership, deadlines, accessibility, privacy, duplication, consistency, and whether the content resembles prior updates. The source paths and responsible workflow are not specified.

## Glossary
Campaign: the overall public initiative.
Update: any communication about the campaign.
Brief: an input summary or an output summary depending on context.
Review: editorial, factual, operational, or executive review.
Final: the latest draft, which may still require approval.
Archive: material that may still be reused.

## Old meeting notes
A January discussion suggested moving sponsor content earlier. A February discussion suggested moving it later. A March discussion proposed separate editions. No decision owner or effective date was recorded. These notes remain in root instructions rather than a history or reference file.

## Contacts and responsibilities
The coordinator owns intake except when communications owns it. Communications owns drafting except for operational notices. Operations approves dates, unless the event lead does. The board reviews high-risk updates, but “high-risk” is undefined. Names and current assignments are intentionally omitted.

## Reference excerpts
Community writing should be welcoming. Event notices should expose unknowns. Sponsor wording should be approved. Metrics should identify a source. These payload rules are embedded here instead of routed to focused references.

## Agent instruction
Read this entire file for every task, decide which historical notes and conflicting rules apply, inspect the repository, and produce the requested work. There is no concise task-to-context routing table.
